authors_text: Yimin Dou,Kewen Li,Hongjie Duan,Timing Li,Lin Dong,Zongchao Huang
title: MDA GAN: Adversarial-Learning-based 3-D Seismic Data Interpolation and Reconstruction for Complex Missing
title_zh: MDA GAN：基于对抗学习的 3D 地震数据插值和复杂缺失重建
comment: This work has been submitted to journal for possible publication
comments: This work has been submitted to journal for possible publication
abstract: The interpolation and reconstruction of missing traces is a crucial step in seismic data processing, moreover it is also a highly ill-posed problem, especially for complex cases such as high-ratio random discrete missing, continuous missing and missing in fault-rich or salt body surveys. These complex cases are rarely mentioned in current works. To cope with complex missing cases, we propose Multi-Dimensional Adversarial GAN (MDA GAN), a novel 3-D GAN framework. It keeps anisotropy and spatial continuity of the data after 3D complex missing reconstruction using three discriminators. The feature stitching module is designed and embedded in the generator to retain more information of the input data. The Tanh cross entropy (TCE) loss is derived, which provides the generator with the optimal reconstruction gradient to make the generated data smoother and continuous. We experimentally verified the effectiveness of the individual components of the study and then tested the method on multiple publicly available data. The method achieves reasonable reconstructions for up to 95% of random discrete missing and 100 traces of continuous missing. In fault and salt body enriched surveys, MDA GAN still yields promising results for complex cases. Experimentally it has been demonstrated that our method achieves better performance than other methods in both simple and complex cases.https://github.com/douyimin/MDA_GAN
summary: The interpolation and reconstruction of missing traces is a crucial step in seismic data processing, moreover it is also a highly ill-posed problem, especially for complex cases such as high-ratio random discrete missing, continuous missing and missing in fault-rich or salt body surveys. These complex cases are rarely mentioned in current works. To cope with complex missing cases, we propose Multi-Dimensional Adversarial GAN (MDA GAN), a novel 3-D GAN framework. It keeps anisotropy and spatial continuity of the data after 3D complex missing reconstruction using three discriminators. The feature stitching module is designed and embedded in the generator to retain more information of the input data. The Tanh cross entropy (TCE) loss is derived, which provides the generator with the optimal reconstruction gradient to make the generated data smoother and continuous. We experimentally verified the effectiveness of the individual components of the study and then tested the method on multiple publicly available data. The method achieves reasonable reconstructions for up to 95% of random discrete missing and 100 traces of continuous missing. In fault and salt body enriched surveys, MDA GAN still yields promising results for complex cases. Experimentally it has been demonstrated that our method achieves better performance than other methods in both simple and complex cases.https://github.com/douyimin/MDA_GAN
abstract_zh: 缺失道的插值与重建是地震资料处理中的关键步骤，同时也是一个高度不适定的问题，特别是对于高比例随机离散缺失、连续缺失以及富断层或盐体勘察中的缺失等复杂情况。这些复杂的案例在当前的作品中很少被提及。为了应对复杂的缺失情况，我们提出了多维对抗 GAN（MDA GAN），一种新颖的 3-D GAN 框架。它使用三个判别器进行 3D 复杂缺失重建后保持数据的各向异性和空间连续性。特征拼接模块被设计并嵌入到生成器中以保留输入数据的更多信息。推导了Tanh交叉熵（TCE）损失，为生成器提供了最优的重建梯度，使生成的数据更加平滑和连续。我们通过实验验证了该研究各个组成部分的有效性，然后在多个公开数据上测试了该方法。该方法对高达 95% 的随机离散缺失和 100 条连续缺失痕迹实现了合理重建。在断层和盐体富集调查中，MDA GAN 对于复杂情况仍然能产生有希望的结果。实验证明，我们的方法在简单和复杂的情况下都比其他方法取得了更好的性能。https://github.com/douyimin/MDA_GAN

AI.tldr: 本文提出MDA GAN框架，解决复杂缺失情况下的三维地震数据插值与重建问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理高比率随机离散缺失、连续缺失及断层/盐体区域等复杂缺失场景，导致重建质量不足。
AI.method: 设计包含三个判别器的3D GAN框架，引入特征拼接模块与Tanh交叉熵损失函数，保留数据各向异性与空间连续性。
AI.result: 在95%随机离散缺失及100条连续缺失数据集上实现合理重建，断层/盐体区域表现优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: MDA GAN在简单与复杂缺失场景均优于对比方法，适用于实际地震数据处理需求。

authors_text: Duo Xu,Faramarz Fekri
title: A Framework for Following Temporal Logic Instructions with Unknown Causal Dependencies
title_zh: 遵循具有未知因果关系的时序逻辑指令的框架
abstract: Teaching a deep reinforcement learning (RL) agent to follow instructions in multi-task environments is a challenging problem. We consider that user defines every task by a linear temporal logic (LTL) formula. However, some causal dependencies in complex environments may be unknown to the user in advance. Hence, when human user is specifying instructions, the robot cannot solve the tasks by simply following the given instructions. In this work, we propose a hierarchical reinforcement learning (HRL) framework in which a symbolic transition model is learned to efficiently produce high-level plans that can guide the agent efficiently solve different tasks. Specifically, the symbolic transition model is learned by inductive logic programming (ILP) to capture logic rules of state transitions. By planning over the product of the symbolic transition model and the automaton derived from the LTL formula, the agent can resolve causal dependencies and break a causally complex problem down into a sequence of simpler low-level sub-tasks. We evaluate the proposed framework on three environments in both discrete and continuous domains, showing advantages over previous representative methods.
summary: Teaching a deep reinforcement learning (RL) agent to follow instructions in multi-task environments is a challenging problem. We consider that user defines every task by a linear temporal logic (LTL) formula. However, some causal dependencies in complex environments may be unknown to the user in advance. Hence, when human user is specifying instructions, the robot cannot solve the tasks by simply following the given instructions. In this work, we propose a hierarchical reinforcement learning (HRL) framework in which a symbolic transition model is learned to efficiently produce high-level plans that can guide the agent efficiently solve different tasks. Specifically, the symbolic transition model is learned by inductive logic programming (ILP) to capture logic rules of state transitions. By planning over the product of the symbolic transition model and the automaton derived from the LTL formula, the agent can resolve causal dependencies and break a causally complex problem down into a sequence of simpler low-level sub-tasks. We evaluate the proposed framework on three environments in both discrete and continuous domains, showing advantages over previous representative methods.
abstract_zh: 教导深度强化学习 (RL) 代理在多任务环境中遵循指令是一个具有挑战性的问题。我们认为用户通过线性时序逻辑（LTL）公式定义每个任务。然而，复杂环境中的一些因果关系可能是用户事先未知的。因此，当人类用户指定指令时，机器人无法通过简单地遵循给定的指令来解决任务。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种分层强化学习（HRL）框架，其中学习符号转换模型以有效地生成可以指导智能体有效解决不同任务的高级计划。具体来说，通过归纳逻辑编程（ILP）学习符号转换模型以捕获状态转换的逻辑规则。通过规划符号转换模型和从 LTL 公式派生的自动机的乘积，代理可以解决因果依赖性，并将因果复杂问题分解为一系列更简单的低级子任务。我们在离散和连续领域的三种环境中评估了所提出的框架，显示出比以前的代表性方法的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层强化学习框架，用于解决存在未知因果依赖的时序逻辑指令跟随问题。
AI.motivation: 用户定义的时序逻辑指令可能因环境中的未知因果依赖而难以直接执行，传统方法无法有效处理此类复杂性。
AI.method: 通过归纳逻辑编程学习符号状态转移模型，并结合LTL自动机进行规划，将复杂任务分解为低级子任务。
AI.result: 在三个离散和连续领域环境中验证，证明框架优于现有代表性方法。
AI.conclusion: 该框架能有效处理未知因果依赖，提升多任务环境下指令跟随的效率与准确性。

authors_text: Bin Li,Ruofeng Wei,Jiaqi Xu,Bo Lu,Chi-Hang Yee,Chi-Fai Ng,Pheng-Ann Heng,Qi Dou,Yun-Hui Liu
title: 3D Perception based Imitation Learning under Limited Demonstration for Laparoscope Control in Robotic Surgery
title_zh: 基于有限演示的 3D 感知模仿学习用于机器人手术中的腹腔镜控制
comment: 7 pages, 7 figures, 2022 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: 7 pages, 7 figures, 2022 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Automatic laparoscope motion control is fundamentally important for surgeons to efficiently perform operations. However, its traditional control methods based on tool tracking without considering information hidden in surgical scenes are not intelligent enough, while the latest supervised imitation learning (IL)-based methods require expensive sensor data and suffer from distribution mismatch issues caused by limited demonstrations. In this paper, we propose a novel Imitation Learning framework for Laparoscope Control (ILLC) with reinforcement learning (RL), which can efficiently learn the control policy from limited surgical video clips. Specially, we first extract surgical laparoscope trajectories from unlabeled videos as the demonstrations and reconstruct the corresponding surgical scenes. To fully learn from limited motion trajectory demonstrations, we propose Shape Preserving Trajectory Augmentation (SPTA) to augment these data, and build a simulation environment that supports parallel RGB-D rendering to reinforce the RL policy for interacting with the environment efficiently. With adversarial training for IL, we obtain the laparoscope control policy based on the generated rollouts and surgical demonstrations. Extensive experiments are conducted in unseen reconstructed surgical scenes, and our method outperforms the previous IL methods, which proves the feasibility of our unified learning-based framework for laparoscope control.
summary: Automatic laparoscope motion control is fundamentally important for surgeons to efficiently perform operations. However, its traditional control methods based on tool tracking without considering information hidden in surgical scenes are not intelligent enough, while the latest supervised imitation learning (IL)-based methods require expensive sensor data and suffer from distribution mismatch issues caused by limited demonstrations. In this paper, we propose a novel Imitation Learning framework for Laparoscope Control (ILLC) with reinforcement learning (RL), which can efficiently learn the control policy from limited surgical video clips. Specially, we first extract surgical laparoscope trajectories from unlabeled videos as the demonstrations and reconstruct the corresponding surgical scenes. To fully learn from limited motion trajectory demonstrations, we propose Shape Preserving Trajectory Augmentation (SPTA) to augment these data, and build a simulation environment that supports parallel RGB-D rendering to reinforce the RL policy for interacting with the environment efficiently. With adversarial training for IL, we obtain the laparoscope control policy based on the generated rollouts and surgical demonstrations. Extensive experiments are conducted in unseen reconstructed surgical scenes, and our method outperforms the previous IL methods, which proves the feasibility of our unified learning-based framework for laparoscope control.
abstract_zh: 自动腹腔镜运动控制对于外科医生高效执行手术至关重要。然而，其基于工具跟踪的传统控制方法不考虑手术场景中隐藏的信息，不够智能，而最新的基于监督模仿学习（IL）的方法需要昂贵的传感器数据，并且由于演示有限而导致分布不匹配问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种带有强化学习（RL）的腹腔镜控制（ILLC）新颖的模仿学习框架，它可以有效地从有限的手术视频片段中学习控制策略。特别地，我们首先从未标记的视频中提取手术腹腔镜轨迹作为演示，并重建相应的手术场景。为了充分学习有限的运动轨迹演示，我们提出了形状保持轨迹增强（SPTA）来增强这些数据，并构建一个支持并行 RGB-D 渲染的模拟环境，以强化与环境有效交互的 RL 策略。通过 IL 的对抗性训练，我们根据生成的展示和手术演示获得了腹腔镜控制策略。在看不见的重建手术场景中进行了大量的实验，我们的方法优于以前的IL方法，这证明了我们基于学习的统一腹腔镜控制框架的可行性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于3D感知的模仿学习框架（ILLC），用于在有限示范数据下实现腹腔镜控制，提升机器人手术的智能化水平。
AI.motivation: 传统腹腔镜控制方法依赖工具跟踪且缺乏场景信息，最新模仿学习方法依赖昂贵传感器且存在有限示范数据导致的分布偏差问题。
AI.method: 提出形状保持轨迹增强（SPTA）方法扩充有限示范数据，构建支持并行RGB-D渲染的仿真环境，并通过对抗训练结合强化学习优化控制策略。
AI.result: 在未见过的重建手术场景中，该方法在多项指标上优于现有模仿学习方法，验证了框架有效性。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的统一学习框架能够有效解决有限示范下的腹腔镜控制问题，为机器人手术智能化提供新思路。

search_text: 3D Perception based Imitation Learning under Limited Demonstration for Laparoscope Control in Robotic Surgery 基于有限演示的 3D 感知模仿学习用于机器人手术中的腹腔镜控制 Automatic laparoscope motion control is fundamentally important for surgeons to efficiently perform operations. However, its traditional control methods based on tool tracking without considering information hidden in surgical scenes are not intelligent enough, while the latest supervised imitation learning (IL)-based methods require expensive sensor data and suffer from distribution mismatch issues caused by limited demonstrations. In this paper, we propose a novel Imitation Learning framework for Laparoscope Control (ILLC) with reinforcement learning (RL), which can efficiently learn the control policy from limited surgical video clips. Specially, we first extract surgical laparoscope trajectories from unlabeled videos as the demonstrations and reconstruct the corresponding surgical scenes. To fully learn from limited motion trajectory demonstrations, we propose Shape Preserving Trajectory Augmentation (SPTA) to augment these data, and build a simulation environment that supports parallel RGB-D rendering to reinforce the RL policy for interacting with the environment efficiently. With adversarial training for IL, we obtain the laparoscope control policy based on the generated rollouts and surgical demonstrations. Extensive experiments are conducted in unseen reconstructed surgical scenes, and our method outperforms the previous IL methods, which proves the feasibility of our unified learning-based framework for laparoscope control. 自动腹腔镜运动控制对于外科医生高效执行手术至关重要。然而，其基于工具跟踪的传统控制方法不考虑手术场景中隐藏的信息，不够智能，而最新的基于监督模仿学习（IL）的方法需要昂贵的传感器数据，并且由于演示有限而导致分布不匹配问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种带有强化学习（RL）的腹腔镜控制（ILLC）新颖的模仿学习框架，它可以有效地从有限的手术视频片段中学习控制策略。特别地，我们首先从未标记的视频中提取手术腹腔镜轨迹作为演示，并重建相应的手术场景。为了充分学习有限的运动轨迹演示，我们提出了形状保持轨迹增强（SPTA）来增强这些数据，并构建一个支持并行 RGB-D 渲染的模拟环境，以强化与环境有效交互的 RL 策略。通过 IL 的对抗性训练，我们根据生成的展示和手术演示获得了腹腔镜控制策略。在看不见的重建手术场景中进行了大量的实验，我们的方法优于以前的IL方法，这证明了我们基于学习的统一腹腔镜控制框架的可行性。 本文提出一种基于3D感知的模仿学习框架（ILLC），用于在有限示范数据下实现腹腔镜控制，提升机器人手术的智能化水平。 Bin Li,Ruofeng Wei,Jiaqi Xu,Bo Lu,Chi-Hang Yee,Chi-Fai Ng,Pheng-Ann Heng,Qi Dou,Yun-Hui Liu cs.RO
